beats.narration_text: Sawadee — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: story-film, the skill that turns any short narrative into a phase-gated AI film. One beat, one scene, one narrated line over one clip. Audio is measured first.

props.runningText: one beat = one scene = one narrated line...
output: Phase 3: 3 storyboard stills per scene (pick keeper)
Phase 4: clip per scene, trim to audio

beats.narration_text: The skill has a phase-gated pipeline, two style presets — phone-grounded and cinematic — and a reuse map showing which existing scripts handle each phase. The beat sheet is the contract every phase reads and writes back to. The build status section is honest: three of five phases are not yet complete.

props.title: Five phases. Two presets. One beat sheet.
files: SKILL.md — pipeline + phase gates + build status

beats.narration_text: Five phase-gated steps: generate and measure narration first — that duration is the beat's clock; segment the story into one beat per line; pick one keeper still per scene from three candidates; generate the clip trimmed to the audio; overlay title and captions from word timestamps. At every gate, regenerate only the failing unit — never the whole batch.

props.title: Audio-lock → still-pick → clip-approve → overlay.
props.outputLabel: narrated AI film (title + captions over clips)

phases.label: NARRATE
phases.note: ElevenLabs TTS + measure — real duration = master clock

phases.label: STILLS
phases.note: 3 candidates/scene via Higgsfield — pick keeper, lock GATE 1

phases.label: CLIP
phases.note: image-to-video trimmed to audio — GATE 2 before overlay

phases.label: OVERLAY
phases.note: title card + captions (from word timestamps) in Remotion

props.footerNote: Regenerate only the failing unit at each gate. Stills are cheap; video costs more — never generate video before the still is approved.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: Phase two beat segmentation from Poe's The Raven — three lines, three beats, scene descriptions and camera moves filled.

props.runningText: segmenting into beats — one line per scene...
output: Phase 1: ElevenLabs pending (paid — not run here)

beats.narration_text: Three beats from the opening stanza. Beat one: midnight dreary — scholar at candlelit desk, slow push-in, cinematic preset. Beat two: the tapping — static shot of closed door, shadow at threshold, cinematic. Beat three: the shutter — window thrown open, curtains rush in, wide to tight as it opens. Each beat holds one line. Phase one audio pending — requires ElevenLabs, out of scope for the free pipeline demo.

props.title: BEATS — Poe 'The Raven' (1845)
props.code:
B01 narration: "Once upon a midnight dreary,
  while I pondered, weak and weary,"
  scene: scholar at candlelit desk, night
  action: figure slumped, candle flickers
  camera: slow push-in to face
  style: cinematic (chiaroscuro)

B02 narration: "Suddenly there came a tapping,
  as of some one gently rapping,"
  scene: closed chamber door, candlelight
  action: shadow moves under door
  camera: static, door in foreground

B03 narration: "Open here I flung the shutter..."
  scene: window thrown open, stormy night
  action: curtains rush in, figure leans out
  camera: wide → tight as shutter opens

Phase 1 audio: pending (ElevenLabs — paid)

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: the narration is the master clock. Every downstream phase derives its timing from the real spoken duration of each beat. A word-count estimate would mean the clip is generated at a guessed length — and then the audio doesn't fit it. The word-level timestamps from Phase one also give caption timing for free: no alignment step, no drift.

props.heading: Measure first. Build second.
props.body: ElevenLabs audio is generated and measured before any visual work begins. actual_duration_s is written to beat_sheet.json; every downstream phase reads that field. Clip length, cut timing, caption timing all come from the same source: the real spoken duration. Word-level timestamps from Phase 1 give the caption overlay perfect timing — no Whisper alignment, no drift.
props.quote: The narration is the master clock.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: phase gates and the single-unit regeneration rule. The pipeline separates the cheap phase — three storyboard stills at low cost — from the expensive one — a video clip. If a still fails, regenerate that still only. If a clip fails, regenerate that clip only. The gate stops the expensive phase from starting until the cheap phase is approved. Never regenerate the whole batch.

props.heading: Gates: cheap before expensive.
props.body: Phase order is cost order: narration (cheap) → stills (cheap) → video (expensive) → overlay (cheap). Gate 1 locks after stills are picked; video is never generated until a keeper still is approved. Gate 2 locks after clips are approved; overlay never starts on a rejected clip. Regenerate only the failing unit — not the batch that passed.
props.quote: one beat = one scene = one narrated line over one generated clip.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: the skill documents its own incomplete state. Three of five phases are not yet built — the multi-reference still runner, the Phase four video trim runner, and the Phase five overlay. The build status section says this plainly. A skill that claims to be finished when it is not produces silent failures: the user finds out at Phase four spend, not Phase zero read.

props.heading: Honest build status.
props.body: Story-film documents what works (Phase 1 audio, Phase 2 beat schema, Phase 3 solo-character stills) and what doesn't (multi-ref stills, Phase 4 trim runner, Phase 5 overlay). The annotation is not a caveat — it is the integrity mechanism. Claiming a skill is complete when it isn't means the user discovers the gap at the expensive phase, not at the cheap read.
props.quote: Safe to save as a work-in-progress snapshot; not yet a finished, end-to-end skill.

beats.narration_text: Story-film: audio-first phase gates, single-unit regeneration, and honest build status. The narration clock. The still before the clip. The gate before the spend.

artifactLines: audio measured first — actual_duration_s is the clock
phase gates: still before clip, approve before spend

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run story-film on a short text. Before you start: can every scene be stated as one narrated line? Which phases require paid generation — ElevenLabs for Phase one, Higgsfield for Phase three, Hailuo for Phase four. If you are using the free pipeline, Phase two segmentation is what you can run today.

props.runningText: can each scene be one narrated line?
output: can each scene be stated as one narrated line?
Phase 4: Hailuo video (paid) — run gate 1 first

beats.narration_text: Claude, Narrating. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
